He explains this with the difference between exchange and use value. Use value is seemingly unproblematic in Marx, it is intact, before exchange value comes on stage - pure and simple, without mysterious properties,60 self-same. Derrida rejects this origin of the spectre61 as use value is socially determined as well. There is no pure use, its meaning is socially overdetermined and historically contingent. The fetish of social forms is not autonomisation alone. Language, for one, always confronts social subjects already as a system that functions according to its own rules. Discourses also develop ‘their own momentum of semantic and cultural processes’®2 In this respect Derrida wants to speak of hauntology63 instead of ontology64
The two approaches are, however, only seemingly in contradiction, for the society where power strategies link up in myriad ways to form a social haunting, is in principle nothing other than Marx’s fetish. The modern capitalist society Derrida and Foucault refer to is the ‘first genuinely global type of societal organization in history ...’,(>’> and thus the first to give its meaning to the term ‘socialisation’, which casts its spell over more and more sectors and cannot exist without ‘constantly revolutionising . the relations of production, and with them the whole relations of society’.66 Surplus objectivity is its particular distinguishing feature. Adorno speaks in this context, following Emile Durkheim, of an ‘impenetrability’ through which one is made to experience society as an autonomous objectivity, which has an element of inaccessibility67 The specifically social consists in the imbalance of institutions over people68
Social forms are thus no longer the only ‘impenetrabilities’ and in this respect Derrida and Foucault are right69 What is special about social forms and could strictly speaking be called spectral consists, however, precisely in that relations are not only complex and therefore hard to see through, but cover up that they are social relations and become reified in that sense. This can be shown with Derrida’s example of use value: although it is overdetermined, as there is no unmediated pre-social use, there is actually nothing ‘mysterious’ about the commodity as use value to the extent that it remains obviously natural matter that has been changed through human activity.70 In exchange value this is no longer visible, as it can only present itself in another commodity and is hence reified.
A theory that focuses on the autonomisation of human activity has to pursue this phenomenon more extensively, beginning with autonomisation in general and how it arises from modern capitalist society and subsequently its specific manners of reification or naturalisation. A striking parallel with the gender binary exists, for instance, which is hardly less fittingly described as an ‘embodiment of social relations’ than the social labour of the commodity. The insight into its social construction is here accomplished by another mechanism: that of biologising the social relation, which remains just as impenetrable for everyday behaviour as the commodity fetish. All the autonomisations suggest that modern bourgeois society has no or only very rudimentary procedures for enlightening and collectively governing itself.
Below I will turn to the specifically juridical manner of autonomisation, the fascination with legal theory, which owes its effects to the hybrid character of its normative factuality. In the represented theories it has thus far been explored through the concepts of ‘relative autonomy’ or ‘relative independence’, and ‘internal-external-dialectic’. I will show that this appearance of impenetrability, similarly to exchange value, can be dissolved in a social relation.
2.4	The Legal Form
2.4.1	Subjectivation and Cohesion
The value or commodity form describes a social form in the Marxian tradition. It processes the satisfaction of needs through social labour in capitalist society, which is based upon private and surplus value production. With the legal form Pashukanis then identifies a further social form. For this assumption to hold true, the legal form also has to represent a social relation that becomes autonomous through abstraction and develops its own form. As a form it has to have an independent object-like quality that is removed from the immediate grasp of everyday behaviour, but guides it, thus becoming a technology of cohesion.
I have attempted to show that law plays a crucial role in subjectivation. Here a mechanism similar to that of equating the products of labour takes place. Balbus calls this ‘the common mode of substitution’. The equality of different kinds of labour can only persist by abstracting from their real inequal- 
ity,71 that is ‘if we reduce them to the characteristic they have in common, that of being the expenditure of human labour-power, of human labour in the abstract’.
Legal subjects are also placed in relation to each other as abstract equals. Equality that is socially produced through abstraction is, one can conclude, the central mode of making the incommensurable commensurable. From this perspective it becomes clear that not only economic (class) differences and antagonisms are processed, as was suggested through the narrow debate about the spheres of production and circulation, but differences as such. Moreover, the legal form thus does not fulfil a ‘function’, but produces a result. That this also turns commodity owners into legal subjects and allows for the exchange of commodities is one effect.
Their division makes the social monads incommensurable with each other. While legal practices create their own divisions, they simultaneously organise the recombination of the social body. The equality of legal subjectivity is a way to allow for the connection of respectively individuated members of society, be it through contracts, through laws or through court rulings. ‘Abstract and equal subjects, the atoms of the legal relationship, cannot relate to each other according to principles of “traditional” privilege, but do so by means of contract, which is the formalisation of mutual recognition of equal subjects’, writes China Mieville in the tradition of Pashukanis.72
Thus law not only provides a forum for engaging in conflicts - ‘disputes’, as Pashukanis thought - but constitutes more widely a formal cohesive framework for the individuated monads. What is formal and abstract is in this sense actually closely related to the real fractures of the social division of labour and of individualisation.73 In civil proceedings, for example, social moments, which the dispute of two legal parties is embedded in, are procedurally placed on the agenda, such as questions of minority, legal responsibility, questions of ‘good faith’, of error and of liability. In constitutional processes the relation of the individual and society is made operational and ‘assessed’ according to formal examination criteria. Pashukanis’ statement, that the legal fabric is ‘the succession of subjects linked together by claims on each other ...’,74 can also be reinterpreted accordingly. Through formalised procedures and abstracting norms society is retrospectively brought into social relationships, and diverging 
individuals are woven together into a social fabric. They can, however, only be bound together through the abstract equality of legal subjectivity.
It is also possible to understand Luhmann’s dictum that law is exclusively about normativity from this perspective. Individuals are only normatively placed in relation to each other as equals. Normativity is possible because of processes that abstract from the here and now. Form abstractions make it possible to process social contradictions. Marx has shown this with the example of the exchange process, which functions although the contradictions remain. ‘The further development of the commodity does not abolish these contradictions, but rather provides the form within which they have room to move. This is, in general, the way in which real contradictions are resolved’."3 Contradictions move within the form; a contradiction without synthesis, that is to say - a paradox.
In the mode of the legal subject the subjects treat each other actually as equals amongst equals, those who are ‘free’ to enter into contracts. No matter, whether the contract concerns employment, marriage, rental or sales, in practice abstractly equal parties to the contract have to be counterfactually assumed. In the marriage contract, for example, the hierarchical gender relationship is juridically transformed into one of equals. ‘Capitalist and ‘worker’ treat each other as equals in their legal practices, even if they remain unaware of this at the moment of their actions. Buying bread, for instance, takes place as part of everyday routine activity as a simple exchange of a product of labour against money. Neither do the subjects think about how labour is equated in the money form, nor are they even aware that this creates a contract based on two equal legal subjects. They do not know it, but they are doing it. Their actions are real abstractions or performative acts, which produce the equality of the subjects at this very moment. If the purchase takes place without problem, this is not addressed. Only when this contractual relation is disturbed, is the relationship legally codified. With contracts, which are required to be in writing, the legal coding is present from the outset, but not the performative production of legal subjectivity. This subjectivity is most readily apparent in the case of company forms that are treated as ‘juridical persons’ in private law76 and thus only acquire subject character through the law.”翻译为中文

2.3 社会关系的自主化：物化、自然化及其他
霍内特指出，物化的概念——这是1920年代和1930年代社会批判的主题——可能会经历一次复兴。8 虽然马克思将物化追溯到社会形式，但福柯和德里达提出了一个更全面的社会过程。虽然两人都是从与马克思不同的理论前提出发，即不是从商品生产出发，而是从语言、话语或权力关系出发，他们也得出了社会关系自主化的结论。在福柯的理论中，如前所示，社会中每个角落发展并汇集成更大构成的“纠缠”权力关系的理念使他从一个独立于社会形式可能的抽象过程的社会匿名性出发。因此，这一基本理念与马克思的十分相似。
然而，德里达认为，马克思仅将幽灵性归咎于商品生产，因此它会随着生产方式的变化而消失。9 相比之下，他认为社会化本身会带来幽灵和鬼魅。他用交换价值和使用价值之间的区别来解释这一点。在马克思看来，使用价值似乎是无问题的，它是完好无损的，在交换价值出现之前——纯粹且简单，没有神秘属性，60 是相同的自身。德里达拒绝了幽灵的这一起源，61 因为使用价值也是社会决定的。没有纯粹的使用，它的意义是社会过度决定的和历史偶然的。社会形式的物化不仅仅是自主化。语言，例如，始终作为一个根据其自身规则运作的系统面对社会主体。话语也发展出“语义和文化过程的自身动力”。62 在这方面，德里达想要谈论的是诡影学，63 而不是本体论。64
然而，这两种方法似乎只是矛盾的，因为其中权力战略以千万种方式链接形成社会幽灵的社会，原则上与马克思的物化没有什么不同。德里达和福柯所指的现代资本主义社会是“历史上第一种真正全球性的社会组织类型……”，65 因此是第一个赋予“社会化”一词意义的，它对越来越多的领域施加魅力，并且不能没有“不断革命化……生产关系及其伴随的整个社会关系”。66 过剩客观性是其特别的区别特征。阿多诺在这个背景下引用埃米尔·涂尔干的话谈论“不可渗透性”，通过这种不可渗透性，人被迫将社会体验为一个自主的客观性，这具有一种难以接近的元素。67 特别的社会性在于机构在人们之上的失衡。68
因此，社会形式不再是唯一的“不透明性”，在这方面德里达和福柯是正确的。69 然而，社会形式的特别之处，严格来说可以称为幽灵性的，正好在于关系不仅复杂因此难以透视，而且掩盖了它们是社会关系并在这个意义上变得具化。这可以用德里达的使用价值示例来展示：尽管它是过度决定的，因为没有未经媒介的预社会使用，商品作为使用价值实际上没有什么“神秘”，因为它显然是通过人类活动改变的自然物质。70 在交换价值中这不再可见，因为它只能在另一种商品中呈现，因此变得具化。
专注于人类活动自主化的理论必须广泛地追求这一现象，从一般的自主化开始，探索它是如何从现代资本主义社会中产生，并随后探索其特定的具化或自然化方式。例如，性别二元性的并列，与商品的社会劳动一样准确地被描述为“社会关系的体现”也并不差。对其社会构建的洞察是通过另一种机制完成的：将社会关系生物化，这对日常行为来说同样是不透明的，就像商品物化一样。所有的自主化表明，现代资产阶级社会没有或只有非常基本的启蒙和集体治理自己的程序。
下面我将转向法律自主化的具体方式，这种方式因其规范事实性的混合特征而对法理论具有吸引力。在所呈现的理论中，迄今已通过“相对自主性”或“相对独立性”和“内外辨证法”的概念进行了探索。我将展示这种不可渗透性的表象，与交换价值类似，可以在社会关系中被解消。
2.4 法律形式
2.4.1 主体化和凝聚
价值或商品形式描述了马克思传统中的一种社会形式。它通过资本主义社会中的社会劳动处理需求的满足，这是基于私有和剩余价值生产的。通过法律形式帕舒卡尼斯随后识别了另一种社会形式。为了使这一假设成立，法律形式也必须代表一种通过抽象变得自主并发展出自己形式的社会关系。作为一种形式，它必须具有独立的物质性质，这种质量脱离了日常行为的直接把握，但却指导它，从而成为一种凝聚技术。
我试图展示法律在主体化中扮演着关键角色。这里发生了一种类似于等同化劳动产品的机制。巴尔布斯称这为“共同的替代模式”。不同类型劳动的平等只能通过从它们真实的不平等中抽象出来才能持续存在，71 即“如果我们将它们归纳为它们共有的特征，即作为人类劳动力的支出，即抽象的人类劳动”。
法律主体也被置于彼此之间作为抽象平等的关系中。可以得出结论，通过抽象产生的社会平等是使不可比的可比的中心模式。从这个角度来看，不仅经济（阶级）差异和对立得以处理，正如通过关于生产和流通领域的狭隘辩论所暗示的那样，而且差异本身也得以处理。而且，法律形式因此并不履行“功能”，而是产生了一个结果。这也使得商品所有者变成了法律主体，并允许商品交换，这是一个影响。
他们的分裂使得社会单体无法相互比较。虽然法律实践创造了它们自己的分裂，但它们同时组织了社会体的重新组合。法律主体性的平等是一种允许相应个体化的社会成员相互联系的方式，无论是通过合同、通过法律还是通过法庭裁决。正如帕舒卡尼斯传统中的中国·米维尔所写，“抽象和平等的主体，法律关系的原子，不能根据‘传统’特权的原则相互关联，而是通过合同来实现，这是对平等主体相互认可的形式化。”72
因此，法律不仅提供了一个参与冲突的论坛——就像帕舒卡尼斯所认为的“争议”——而且更广泛地构成了个体化单体的正式凝聚框架。在这种意义上，形式和抽象实际上与社会劳动分工和个体化的实际裂痕密切相关。73 例如，在民事诉讼中，两个法律方当事人争议嵌入的社会时刻，如少数群体、法律责任、“善意”、错误和责任问题，在程序上被提上议程。在宪法程序中，个人与社会的关系得以操作化，并根据正式审查标准“评估”。帕舒卡尼斯的说法，即法律结构是“通过对彼此主张的主体相互联系的连续性……”，74 也可以相应地重新解释。通过形式化程序和抽象规范，社会事后被带入社会关系中，分歧的个体被编织成一个社会结构。然而，它们只能通过法律主体性的抽象平等联系在一起。
也可以从这个角度理解卢曼的论断，即法律仅涉及规范性。个体仅作为平等者在规范上相互关联。由于抽象过程偏离了此时此地，因此规范性成为可能。形式抽象使得处理社会矛盾成为可能。马克思已经用交换过程的例子展示了这一点，尽管矛盾仍然存在，但交换过程仍然能够运作。“商品的进一步发展并没有消除这些矛盾，而是提供了一个它们有移动空间的形式。这通常是解决实际矛盾的方式。”75 矛盾在形式内移动；换句话说，一个没有综合的矛盾——即悖论。
以法律主体的方式，主体实际上将彼此视为平等中的平等者，那些“自由”签订合同的人。无论合同涉及雇佣、婚姻、租赁还是销售，在实践中必须反事实地假设合同双方是抽象平等的。例如，在婚姻合同中，等级制的性别关系在法律上被转化为平等关系。“资本家和‘工人’在他们的法律实践中将彼此视为平等，即使在他们行动的那一刻他们对此并不知情。例如，购买面包作为劳动产品对钱的简单交换，作为日常例行活动的一部分进行。主体既不会思考劳动是如何在货币形式中等同的，也不会意识到这是基于两个平等法律主体的合同。他们不知道，但他们正在这样做。他们的行动是真实的抽象或表现行为，正是在这一刻产生了主体的平等。如果购买没有问题，这就不会被提及。只有当这种合同关系受到干扰时，才会将关系法律化。对于需要书面形式的合同，法律编码从一开始就存在，但并不是表现性产生法律主体性。这种主体性在以“法人”身份在私法中被处理的公司形式中最为明显，从而仅通过法律获得主体特征。

请将“This mechanism does not only work in private law. Thus the legal practices of penal law, which centre on the relationship of the individual subject and the authority of the state, assume a responsible, autonomous subject. Or when, for 
example, a lack of responsibility is established, this is explicitly substantiated as a deviation from the norm through an exception rule. The legal subject in its double existence as an empirical, real and imaginary one also constitutes a real abstraction.77 It is also apparent at this point of the analysis that the legal abstractions of, for example, class and gender differences or of the ‘hidden abode of production’ (Marx), the ‘veiling function’ of the law Franz Neumann noted, are expressions of this ‘mode of encoding reality’ (Balbus) and thus a product of those social structures rather than a conspiratorial strategy. Elements of social differentiation are for the law ‘virtually inconceivable’?8
Furthermore, precisely the common mode of abstraction that makes concrete, different individuals compatible is a prerequisite for qualitative antagonisms. Only because everyday agents can encounter each other in law as formally equal and free, do their differences remain structurally covered, and thus unaddressed. Law succeeds, moreover, in presenting itself as an exclusive code of power, as the code of organised public violence, as Foucault shows. At the same time, however - and this has created difficulties for materialist legal theory thus far - as the flipside of abstraction, subjects can also encounter each other as equal and free. Abstraction is a condition for making society possible in its dual meaning. Therefore Andrea Maihofer aptly notes that the abstraction of modern law is both its strength and also its structural limit?9
2.4.2	Reification through Procedures
When the sociality of subjects is expressed in law, it nevertheless remains a purely social imaginary just like the value abstraction. How does this imaginary enter social practice? How is this managed and symbolised? Law gains a material existence (Maihofer) in legal form II,8° that is, in juridical procedures. There only certain juridical arguments can be presented by a body of specialised legal practitioners. Modern law develops co-originally with modern officialdom, that is, a specialised intellectual labour force which is highly qualified and technically trained through long years of preparatory education81 Court cases, legislative and administrative procedures do not leave law to the everyday activities of the subjects, they code those practices juridically. They interpret social reality according to certain rules as legal, as legal communication as 
understood by Luhmann. The individual actors are no longer able to grasp their own conditions, once they have entered those procedures. They include both a specific language and technical knowledge, but potentially also architectural peculiarities, mechanisms of social exclusion, their own time frames and so on, thus leading a ‘life of their own’.82
In engaging with Habermas the central significance of those processes for the modern legal form has become apparent: They sort out ‘issues and contributions, information and reasons in such a way’83 that only such arguments can pass the ‘filters’ of the procedures - these are on the one hand juridical and on the other hand can claim hegemonic validity. They exclude the subalterns and are the classic terrain of legal intellectuals, who master the techniques of legal argument and are juridically disciplined. Poulantzas and Foucault concur that the relationship of intellectual labour and political domination, of knowledge and power, culminates in the capitalist state or the secularised form of pastoral power.84 The political as well as the legal form are on the side of intellectual labour, organise hegemony through it and rule the population.
That juridical procedures require arguments to be put forward makes, according to Habermas’s insight, the communicative contradiction permanent. This thereby transforms that contradiction into a productive forced5 which can lead to social integration. In light of the insights of Gramsci and Foucault, this idea has to be reformulated so that the procedures are technologies of consensus which create consensus in a procedural manner through communicative action. It has to be possible to generalise every argument and to relate it to the context of other arguments.
I had argued with systems theory against Habermas that law is also systemic, or, in the terminology employed here, that it functions in a legal form, but that this does not mean that action is therefore brought to a halt. It only shows what this means concretely: the communicative actors are forced into particular procedures in which their own social relations slip away from them. Not they themselves, but juridical intellectuals argue according to particular techniques. Thus communicative action is not brought to a halt in general, but particular subjects are excluded, although always only on the basis that they accept those exclusionary procedures as well as their rules in their everyday practices and behave accordingly. Indeed, the opposing parties in a civil case can interrupt the procedure and settle according to other considerations of fairness or of a pragmatic character ‘out of court’. However, in doing so the production of recursive legal operations is cut short. Nor can judges in penal cases ignore the rules of court and close the proceedings, because the accused appears ‘innocent to them according to their own moral concepts. Legal form II is also ultimately reproduced by the practice of subjects, if they let themselves be ruled just as they get up every morning, go to work or school, accept the traffic regulations, participate in elections or start families of origin.
Those procedures result in the transformation of original social relations through court decisions, acts, legal codes, street signs (as a specific form of the administrative act), and even deportations and incarcerations. The procedures, which Kirchheimer dismisses as merely ‘technical law’, in which decisions are in fact wrested from the respective social distribution of power and transferred into the legal sphere as well as juridically coded, are here revealed as by no means the end of the legal form, but as its essence. The legal form creates a new reality with its own realm: a counterfactual factuality (Brunkhorst) that seals itself off against direct access. The social relation represented by legal form I is that of subjects to each other. In legal form II it becomes independent in the legal procedures as an ensemble of practices and institutions. The initial social relation is dissolved and reshaped by the processes of the legal form, in which participants become entangled as though in a spider web. Procedures thus become normalising procedures, in which the opposing parties in defending their legal affairs experience the abstraction from their subjectivity: ‘of being written into the relevant doctrinal script, the erasure of their subjectivity in order to become the necessary kind of legal subjects ... Legal subjects are thus locked in the house of law ...’86
The legal subject created in the procedures appears in consequence as though it precedes the law and is merely regulated by it. Its qualities (equality, freedom, autonomy, responsibility) appear as natural qualities. A legal fetishism emerges, which prevents both the awareness of its constructed character and concepts of a society that can reproduce itself without the legal (form). Law no longer appears as a social relation and becomes instead an eternal, quasi- anthropological medium of socialisation. This is how it is reified - by becoming a quality of society as such. What remains invisible here is that this actually corresponds instead to a (form of) socialisation that produces itself surreptitiously.
2.4.3	Relational Autonomy
The autonomisation of social relations is at the same time the condition of their ‘relative autonomy’. The specific socialisation under capitalism creates social forms, ‘economic’ no less than legal or political ones. It is the autonomisation and fetishisation of those relations that make possible a materiality of those forms themselves. For then they can no longer be mere ‘appearance’, ‘insofar as human beings in fact become dependent on those objectivities, which are obscure to them’.87 They are ‘not only false consciousness, but also simultaneously reality ... That the categories of illusion are in truth also categories of reality, this is dialectic’,88 and it already shows in that seeing through this ‘appearance’ changes nothing materially. Thus convicted offenders cannot save themselves from punishment through their awareness of the legal form; contracts have to be followed, even if they are nothing but the reified form of human sociality.
The self-referentiality of law, which in Luhmann differentiates itself into a legal system, is in principle the same discovery. When he claims that the differentiation of an ‘operatively closed legal system’ takes place through the recursive reference of legal operations to legal operations, and accordingly calls it an intentional tautology^9 or when Kelsen anticipates systems theory in evoking the ‘autonomy’ of normative systems^0 they describe the fetishised autonomisation of the legal form.”翻译为中文

这一机制不仅在私法中有效。因此，围绕个体主体与国家权威关系的刑法实践，假设一个负责任、自主的主体。或者，当例如确定缺乏责任时，这被明确地作为通过例外规则偏离常规的证据。法律主体以其双重存在——作为经验的、真实的和想象的——也构成了一种真实的抽象。77 在分析的这一点上也很明显，例如，阶级和性别差异或马克思所说的“生产的隐秘之所”（Marx），法律的“遮蔽功能”如弗朗茨·诺伊曼所注意到的，是这种“现实编码模式”（Balbus）的表达，因此是那些社会结构的产物而不是一种阴谋策略。法律中的社会差异元素“几乎是无法想象的”。78

此外，使具体、不同的个体兼容的共同抽象模式正是定性对立的前提。只有因为日常参与者可以在法律中作为形式上平等和自由的个体相遇，他们的差异才在结构上被掩盖，因此未被提及。法律还成功地将自己展现为权力的独占性代码，如福柯所示，作为有组织的公共暴力的代码。然而，同时——这为唯物主义法律理论迄今造成了困难——作为抽象的反面，主体也可以彼此作为平等和自由的个体相遇。抽象是以双重意义使社会成为可能的条件。因此，安德烈亚·迈霍弗恰当地指出，现代法律的抽象既是其力量也是其结构限制。79

2.4.2 通过程序的物化
当主体的社会性在法律中得以表达时，它仍然像价值抽象一样是纯粹的社会想象。这种想象如何进入社会实践？这是如何管理和象征化的？法律在法律形式 II 中获得物质存在（迈霍弗），即，在司法程序中。只有特定的法律论据可以由专业的法律从业者团体提出。现代法律与现代公务员制度共同发展，即通过长年的准备教育而高度资质和技术训练的专业知识劳动力。81 法院案件、立法和行政程序不把法律留给主体的日常活动，而是将这些实践编码为法律的。它们根据某些规则将社会现实解释为法律，正如卢曼所理解的法律交流。一旦个体参与这些程序，他们就不再能够掌握自己的条件。它们包括特定的语言和技术知识，但也可能包括建筑特点、社会排斥机制、自己的时间框架等，从而过着“自己的生活”。82

通过与哈贝马斯的接触，这些过程对现代法律形式的中心意义变得明显：它们以这样的方式“筛选问题和贡献、信息和理由”，83 使得只有那些能通过程序的“过滤器”的论点——一方面是司法的，另一方面可以声称具有霸权有效性的。它们排除了次要群体，成为法律知识分子的经典领域，他们掌握法律论证技术并受到法律规训。波兰扎斯和福柯一致认为，知识劳动与政治统治的关系、知识与权力的关系，在资本主义国家或世俗化的牧师权力形式中达到顶峰。84 政治形式和法律形式位于知识劳动的一边，通过它组织霸权并统治人口。

根据哈贝马斯的洞察，司法程序要求提出论点，使沟通矛盾成为永久性的。这使得矛盾转化为一种生产力，85 可以导致社会整合。鉴于格拉姆西和福柯的见解，这个想法必须被重新表述，以便程序是通过沟通行动以程序化方式创造共识的共识技术。每个论点都必须能够被概括并与其他论点的背景联系起来。
我曾经用系统理论反对哈贝马斯主张，法律也是系统性的，或者用这里的术语，它以法律形式运作，但这并不意味着行动因此而停止。这只说明了这具体意味着什么：沟通的行为者被迫进入特定的程序，其中他们自己的社会关系从他们手中溜走。不是他们自己，而是法律知识分子根据特定技术进行论证。因此，沟通行动并没有总体停止，但特定主体被排除，尽管总是基于他们接受那些排他性程序及其规则在日常实践中并据此行动的基础上。事实上，民事案件的对立方可以中断程序，并根据其他公平或实用性考虑“庭外和解”。然而，在这样做时，递归的法律操作的产生被中断。在刑事案件中，法官也不能无视法庭规则并结束程序，因为被告根据他们自己的道德观念显得“无辜”。法律形式 II 也最终通过主体的实践再生产，如果他们像他们每天早上起床、去工作或学校、接受交通规则、参加选举或建立原生家庭一样让自己被统治。

这些程序导致通过法院决定、法令、法典、路标（作为行政行为的特定形式）甚至驱逐和监禁，原始社会关系的转化。基尔海默轻视为仅仅是“技术性法律”的程序，实际上是从相应的社会权力分配中夺取决定并转移到法律领域以及被法律编码的过程，并在这里显示出绝不是法律形式的终结，而是其本质。法律形式用其本身的领域创造了一个新的现实：一个封闭对抗直接接触的反事实事实性（布伦克霍斯特）。法律形式 I 所代表的社会关系是主体之间的关系。在法律形式 II 中，它在法律程序中作为实践和机构的集合独立开来。最初的社会关系被法律形式的过程所解散和重塑，参与者变得像困在蜘蛛网中一样。程序因此成为规范化程序，当中的对立方在维护其法律事务时体验到从其主体性中抽象出来：“被写入相关教义剧本中，他们的主体性被抹去以成为所需的法律主体类型……法律主体因此被锁在法律之家中……”86

因此，在程序中创造的法律主体似乎是在法律之前就存在的，并仅由法律进行规范。其品质（平等、自由、自主、责任）显得是自然品质。法律物化主义出现了，阻碍了对其构建性质的认识以及没有法律（形式）就可以自我再生产的社会概念。法律不再显现为一种社会关系，而是变成了一种永恒的、准人类学的社会化媒介。这就是它如何被物化的——成为社会本身的一种品质。这里看不见的是，这实际上相反地对应于一种（形式的）悄然自行产生的社会化。
2.4.3 关系性自主
社会关系的自主化同时也是它们“相对自主”的条件。资本主义下的特定社会化创造了社会形式，“经济”形式不亚于法律或政治形式。正是这些关系的自主化和物化使这些形式本身的物质性成为可能。因为那时它们就不再仅仅是“表象”，“因为人们实际上变得依赖于那些对他们来说模糊不清的客体”。87 它们“不仅是错误的意识，而且同时也是现实……错觉的范畴也是现实的范畴，这就是辩证法”，88 这已经显示在看透这种“表象”并不会在物质上改变任何东西。因此，被判罪的罪犯无法通过他们对法律形式的认识来免受惩罚；合同必须被遵守，即使它们只不过是人类社会性的具化形式。

法律的自我指涉性，在卢曼那里分化为法律系统，原则上是同一种发现。当他声称“运作封闭的法律系统”的分化是通过法律操作对法律操作的递归引用而发生，并因此称之为有意的同义反复^9 或者当凯尔森在唤起规范系统的“自主性”时预见了系统理论^0 他们描述了法律形式的物化自主化。

请将“Form analysis makes clear that social ‘subsystems’ do not have to be ‘derived’ in a functionalist way. The law does not exist in order to fulfil a social function (be that the exchange of commodities, the stabilising of expectations or the production of a united power bloc). It is rather possible to genealogically trace how social forms develop as a result of the autonomisation of social conditions, as they gain a certain stability over a particular period and are initially removed from immediate action and thus also change through their fetishised form. That they also process social contradictions in the course of this and thus provide the form those contradictions can move within, is the result of a process of searching and the condition for their continued existence, but not their assigned performance.
This autonomy is, however, relative. Autonomy and relativity initially appear as irreconcilable contradictions. That is indeed the case, but only for a non- dialectical theory. The dialectic thinks the presence of the different up to the 
opposite in the respectively other or the unity of the opposed.91 The autonomy of the law is historically specific, the result of the autonomisation of social conditions. As such it is, however, always already an element of the social totality. The law is only autonomous in abstraction from this totality, but relative in relation to the other practices and conditions or power technologies and in particular to the social context. ‘Relative’ is time and again misunderstood as ‘some shaded concept of autonomy’,92 that is, as one that discusses a greater or lesser degree of autonomy. This is then at the same time rejected in a dualist manner as a logical contradiction. Even Luhmann is ultimately defeated by this concept and remains committed to a Kantian idea of dialectics, that is, a negatively connoted one of antinomies, where there is nothing but either/or. The dialectics that go back to Hegel, on the other hand, start from the ‘absolutely mediated nature of every existing thing’,93 - a pure autonomy, no matter how much it might be backed up by autopoiesis,94 is accordingly a mere function - a ‘stop to reflection’. However, in order to prevent the possibility of biased misunderstandings, I will in what follows no longer talk about relative, but about relational autonomy?5
It is also its own density that is central for the question of whether a social norm is part of the legal technology or whether it is only ‘a mock-up of legality’. Only if the legal form is present in its relational autonomy, which makes direct access even for powerful social actors impossible or at least much harder, can one speak in bourgeois-capitalist societies with regards to social norms of ‘law’. Precisely this is the basis for the distinction conceptualised by Franz Neumann. The legal form is always already, as Foucault has shown, embedded in a correlation system of power technologies. Other technologies insert themselves into its niches and overdetermine it. But it retains nonetheless a relational autonomy with regard to those techniques. Only when this is no longer the case, for instance when immediate relations of power, in the form of the old, repressive form of power, let it implode, is its ‘autonomy’ destroyed and this technology of power eroded. The concrete conditions for generating relational autonomy all have to be researched in their historic specificity. The decisive question is whether a certain right has the possibility to develop according to 
its own internal logic without permanent challenge to its independent reproduction. A third entity outside the parties in conflict (ordinarily a court) is a conditio sine qua non. However, the monopoly on coercive force cannot be held responsible for autonomisation a priori. It is one central condition for making it possible, but not the only one. It is even more decisive that the law is socially practised, as Maihofer rightly stresses.
Furthermore, the autonomisation of the legal institutions explains why law according to Neumann’s conclusions has ‘two sides’ as it offers at least legal possibilities to the weak.96 It is a material condensation of the relationship of forces. For Poulantzas’ problem of founding relational autonomy, this means also that precisely this fetishised, reified independence of the social forms brings its own logic and resistance to social power relations with it. Even the most powerful actors do not dispose of the social forms, but also have to move within them.
2.4.4	Out of the Legal Form
If the understanding of Luhmann’s systems theory advanced in Part 1 is correct, which is that, although affirmative, it is an altogether appropriate description of the reification of social relations, it can as an immanent description help here to represent the concrete mechanisms of law’s own density. It thus provides an answer to the question of how autonomisation is actually operationalised, and how it prevents as legal form a transparent and immediately accessible law.
2.4.4.1	Self-reproduction
Firstly ‘operative closedness’ - understood as a quasi-systemic isolation of the legal form - is a mode of impermeability. In contrast to the normative- democratic concepts of Habermas and Maus, who envisage an internal hierarchy of legal acts with the democratic legislature at the top, the really existing autonomisation of the legal form is marked by the circular (‘hyper-cyclical’) interlinking of legal acts with each other. This leads to a creation of legislation internally within law, which itself brings forth law respectively through binary coding, executing programmes and legal validity.
In doing so, it independently produces, articulates and reflects the components of the legal form, so that the sovereignty of the people is thwarted and at most still serves as a self-description for enabling new linkages within the legal. The practice of law-producing actors has only little in common with the everyday understanding of a self-organised society. Permanent shifts in the exercise 
of applying law and of jurisprudence occur that are almost euphemistically described by the term ‘legal development’. Thus the constitutional ties of state apparatuses to ‘programming through the base’ (Maus) fall victim to this auto-nomisation. The structural reason for modern law’s resistance to the rule of law and democracy lies in the autonomisation that characterises social forms and at the same time makes this process invisible. For legal discourses become inaccessible for extra-legal moments. When it is legally coded, the extra-legal world is incorporated in legal practice. ‘Untamed thinking outside the law cannot claim any relevance’, Luhmann aptly writes.97 Only through filters can it find its way into law, and always at the price that legally incompatible moments are selected.
Here the second effect of autonomisation also already shows itself: The legal form provides a deferral, a delay and additional difficulty for asserting immediate claims to power98 This duality is the reason for the ambivalent character of law, the ‘intersection of emancipation and violence’.99
At the same time this is how abstraction from socially produced and hierarchised differences functions in actu. Only the legal construction of the legal subject as formally equal, free and autonomous is processed in the legal form, not the differences. If the attempt is made to nonetheless include them in law, this can only take place incompletely and again with legal mechanisms, such as the reverse onus clause in favour of the materially weaker litigant,100 or the ‘Third Party Effect of Fundamental Rights’, for example as ‘shining through’ subconstitutional general clauses, so that they can also be applied against powerful private parties to a dispute and no longer only asserted against the state. Thus contracts, for one, are considered immoral, if a structural inequality is exploited in their conclusion.101
2.44.2 Dogma
Within the process of autonomisation the juridical method, called dogmatics in the legal system of continental Europe, plays a decisive role. This ‘selfdescription’ only made self-referentiality and the construction of an order possible, as Teubner has shown. Moreover, individual cases are thus situated in the context of previous and subsequent distinctions. It assumes that cases have been decided before and that others will follow after.102 For this recursive network to be recognised and a coordination of decisions to become possible, a certain redundancy is necessary, a formulaic character and reproducibility. Dogmatics provides for relative consistency in the relations of myriad decisions to each other. Thus the risk of disintegration through the sheer number of individual decisions that have nothing to say to each other - and this could make the paradox of the legal form visible - is normalised:
With the technique of subsumption, the style of expert opinion, the establishment of a ‘prevailing view’ and the disciplining of the juridical subjects.103 From the eighteenth and nineteenth century on this structural framework, this web of points of view, developed.W4
Robert Alexy describes the way dogmatics works in the following way: First it has a stabilising effect, that is, certain solutions, once found, are fixed over long periods and thus rendered reproducible: ‘If one were to reopen the discussion at any time the possibility would arise that ... one might arrive at different outcomes’^5 Furthermore it has a burden-reducing effect: unless there is some special ground, fresh testing of the legal form is unnecessary^6 it can be used in routinised activity. 形式分析明确表明，社会“子系统”不必以功能主义的方式被“衍生”。法律的存在并不是为了履行社会功能（无论是商品交换、稳定预期还是形成统一的权力集团）。而是可以通过系谱追踪来追溯社会形式如何作为社会条件自主化的结果发展而来，因为它们在特定时期获得了一定的稳定性，并最初从直接行动中被移除，因此也通过其物化形式发生了改变。它们在此过程中处理社会矛盾，并因此提供矛盾可以运动的形式，是寻求的结果和它们持续存在的条件，而不是它们被分配的表现。

然而，这种自主性是相对的。自主性和相对性最初似乎是不可调和的矛盾。确实如此，但仅适用于非辩证法理论。辩证法思考不同直到对立在相应的其他或对立的统一中的存在。91 法律的自主性是历史特定的，是社会条件自主化的结果。作为这样，它总是已经是社会整体的一个元素。法律只是在从这一整体抽象出来时才是自主的，但与其他实践和条件或权力技术特别是与社会背景相比则是相对的。“相对”一词一再被误解为“某种阴影概念的自主性”，92 即，作为一个讨论自主性更大或更小程度的概念。然后同时以二元论方式作为逻辑矛盾而被拒绝。即使是卢曼最终也被这一概念击败，并仍致力于康德式的辩证法观念，即反义词对的负面意义，那里只有非此即彼。另一方面，回溯到黑格尔的辩证法，从“每一存在事物的绝对中介性”出发，93 - 纯粹的自主性，无论它可能有多少自组织支持，94 因此只是一个功能 - 一个“反思的终止”。然而，为了防止可能的误解，下文中我将不再谈论相对，而是谈论关系性自主。95

对于是否一个社会规范是法律技术的一部分，或者它只是“法律性的模型”的问题，其自身的密度是至关重要的。只有当法律形式以其关系性自主存在，即使对于强大的社会行为者也使直接接触变得不可能或至少困难得多时，才能在资产阶级-资本主义社会中将社会规范称为“法律”。正是这一点是弗朗茨·诺伊曼所概念化的区别的基础。法律形式总是已经，如福柯所示，嵌入在权力技术的关联系统中。其他技术插入其缝隙并使其过度决定。但是它仍然保留与这些技术的关系性自主。只有当这种情况不再存在时，例如当旧的、压迫性的权力形式以直接的权力关系形式让它内爆时，它的“自主性”就被摧毁了，这种权力技术就被侵蚀了。生成关系性自主的具体条件都必须根据其历史特定性进行研究。决定性的问题是某个特定权利是否有可能根据其自身的内部逻辑发展，而不是对其独立再现的持续挑战。一个第三方（通常是一个法院）是冲突双方之外的必要条件。然而，强制力量的垄断不能预先负责自主化。它是使之成为可能的一个核心条件，但不是唯一的条件。更为决定性的是法律被社会实践，正如迈霍弗正确强调的。

此外，法律制度的自主化解释了为什么根据诺伊曼的结论，法律有“两面性”，因为它至少为弱者提供了法律可能性。96 它是力量关系的物质凝聚。对于波兰扎斯关系性自主的问题，这也意味着正是这种物化、具化的社会形式的独立性使它自身的逻辑和对社会权力关系的抵抗力随之而来。即使是最强大的行为者也不能支配社会形式，但也必须在它们中运动。

仅当卢曼的系统理论中推进的理解是正确的，尽管是肯定的，但它是对社会关系物化的全然恰当的描述，那么，作为一种内在描述，它可以帮助在这里代表法律自身密度的具体机制。因此，它为自主化实际如何运作以及法律形式如何阻止法律透明和直接可及的问题提供了答案。

2.44.1 自我再生
首先，“运作封闭性”——理解为法律形式的准系统性隔离——是一种不可渗透性模式。与哈贝马斯和麦斯设想的内部法律行为层次结构相反，其中民主立法位于顶端，真实存在的法律形式的自主化被法律行为之间的循环（“超循环”）相互联系的标记所标记。这导致法律内部产生立法，通过二元编码、执行程序和法律有效性自行产生法律。
在这个过程中，它独立地产生、表述和反映法律形式的组成部分，以至于人民的主权被挫败，最多还能作为促进法律内部新联系的自我描述。法律生产者的实践与自组织社会的日常理解几乎没有共通之处。应用法律和法学实践的永久性转移几乎是用“法律发展”这个词委婉地描述的。从而，“通过基础编程”（麦斯）将国家机构与宪法绑定的理念成为了这种自主化的牺牲品。现代法律对法治和民主的抵抗的结构性原因在于，自主化特征标志着社会形式，并同时使这一过程变得不可见。因为法律话语对外部法律时刻变得不可接近。当它被法律编码时，外部法律世界被纳入法律实践中。“未被驯服的法外思维不能声称具有任何相关性”，卢曼恰当地写道。97 只有通过过滤器才能找到进入法律的方式，且总是以选择不兼容法律时刻为代价。

在这里，自主化的第二个效应也已经显现：法律形式提供了延缓、延迟和额外困难，以实现直接的权力要求98 这种二元性是法律的复杂性格——“解放与暴力的交汇处”的原因。99

同时，这也是在实践中抽象于社会产生的、等级化差异的功能。只有法律中对法律主体的法律构建——作为形式上平等、自由和自主的——得到处理，而不是差异。如果尝试仍然将它们纳入法律，这只能进行不完全地并再次通过法律机制进行，例如，以对物质较弱的当事人有利的推定责任条款，100 或“基本权利的第三方效力”为例，例如通过“透过”次宪法的一般条款“照耀”，以便它们也可以针对强大的私人争议方施加，而不再仅仅针对国家。因此，例如，如果在结构不平等的基础上进行剥削，则合同被视为不道德。101

2.44.2 教条
在自主化过程中，大陆欧洲法律系统中称为教条学的法律方法发挥了决定性作用。正如泰布纳所展示的，这种“自我描述”才使得自我指涉性和秩序构建成为可能。此外，个案因此被置于之前和之后区分的背景中。它假设之前已经有案件被裁决过，之后还会有其他案件。102 为了识别这种递归网络并使决策协调成为可能，需要一定的冗余性、公式化特征和可复制性。教条学为无数决策之间的相对一致性提供了保障。从而通过大量个别决策的风险——这些决策彼此之间没有任何联系——这可能使法律形式的悖论变得可见——得以规范化：通过归纳技术、专家意见风格、建立“主流观点”和对法律主体的规范。103 从十八和十九世纪开始，这种结构框架、这种观点网络发展起来。104
罗伯特·阿列克西以以下方式描述教条学的工作方式：首先它具有稳定作用，即一旦找到某些解决方案，就会在很长一段时间内固定下来，从而使其可复制：“如果随时重新开放讨论，就有可能......得出不同的结果”。105 此外，它具有减轻负担的效果：除非有一些特殊理由，否则无需重新测试法律形式。106 它可以在例行活动中使用。此外，它使不断增加的差异化和根据它们之间的依赖关系简化、系统化地呈现法律规范成为可能。107 最后，它保存了多种解决方案模型，这些模型对于重新开始的人来说是想不到的。108 因此，它将自己与独立和无限的（法律政策）质疑隔离开来，并充当“在寻求理由的过程中为推理设定限制的规则”。109

At this point one encounters within the legal form its paradox, the contradiction it processes: The juridical argumentation is a deparadoxifying regulation. The autonomous character of the legal form rests on uncertain ground. What is legal and thus conversely illegal, is not predetermined by the legal form itself, but the result of hegemonic struggles, which are however normalised through the formal coercion of dogmatics. Hegemonic leadership is inscribed in this form of argument. In particular when one asks why precisely this specific argument prevails in jurisprudence and not another one, for instance how a ‘right to the enterprise’ can be argued for, but not a ‘right to work’, the political character of the legal form is revealed. The decisions based upon power, which are ultimately secured by figures of argumentation, are by no means arbitrary. They follow on the one hand the dogmatic requirements of the legal form and secondly the hegemonic projects which can be universalised. Thus they are anything but the arbitrary decisions of individual agents of the state.
Formally the inscription of hegemony shows in that the consistency of the dogmatic arguments always only exists in relation, according to the relational autonomy of the legal form. For the arguments are essentially based on their repetition of previous arguments - an identical repetition is, however, impossible. Here hegemonic shifts can thus already take place. The questioning cannot be stopped in the radical manner envisaged by Luhmann, who stopped at a ‘literalness of communication theory’110 and postulated the explanation as the correct exegesis of a text in Old-European style. Dogmatic justifications rather give birth to texts, and texts increase the opportunity to say ‘no’.m Hegemonic decisions only solve the paradox temporarily. In antagonistic struggles a network of social actors thus manages to consensually universalise a particular world view, which can inscribe itself in the legal form. Yet, due to the antagonistic structure of the decision the counter-hegemonic project is always already being planned: ‘Every hegemonic order is susceptible of being challenged by counter-hegemonic practices, i.e. practices which will attempt to disarticulate the existing order so as to install another form of hegemony’.n2
Hegemonic projects can thus draw on the legal form to the extent that here projects are developed by juridical knowledge workers in particular legal argumentation. The law thus allows for a unification of divergent projects. It provides virtually the ideal type of a formal cohesion framework, because it is designed for universality in its own structure, its abstraction and reliance on formalised justification.113 
2-4-4-4	The Material Support of Autonomisation
In Luhmann’s view, compulsory decision-making and legal force - institutionalised in courts - justifiably support the autonomisation of law. That courts ultimately have to make decisions and at least temporarily actually ‘free’ those decisions from permanent questioning, stems from the fact that the legal form can only prevail if it succeeds in the individual case. For it is only then that the recursive network of legal operations reaches closure. An unapplied law would leave the circle at some point. Therefore jurisdiction did not gain a special position within the legal system only with Luhmann, but already for Hans Kelsen. He rightly emphasised that ‘one should not overlook the important fact that in the last analysis the law is not what the legislator more or less clearly sets forth or what the rule of custom more or less comprehensibly implies. The law is what the court finally decides’.114 Indeed many, if not most, legal communications take place outside of juridical institutions; however, this occurs only in the material context that ensures that they could always also be accessible to juridical procedures. The legally binding decision ends ‘in the last instance’ - a possibly infinite regress which would destroy the connections between legal discourses and thus make autonomisation impossible.
Obviously those decisions are again new texts, the meaning of which cannot be fixed once and for all but is open to hegemonic practice. However, the decision in the last instance fixes, at least for a historical moment, a particular meaning, so that it can become effective. It ‘freezes’ social relations for a particular moment in time and thus allows for decisions following from it to be developed. A shift in meaning is at first postponed. The prohibition of the denial of justice coupled with legal force is truly the pillar of the legal form. The courts as its institutionalisation are therefore especially significant for the relational autonomy of the law. Without a strong juridical institutionalisation (including extensive rights to sue and jurisdictions) this cannot develop. Powerful actors can instead occupy the meaning of what is ‘legal’ and trigger something of a law of fables [Legendenrecht].
2.44.5	Fetish and Paradox
Managing the paradox of the social forms has the effect of processing social contradictions. The contradiction (the foundational paradox) is precisely not resolved, but maintained. The ‘windowless monads’ are connected without a master plan or steering subject, decentralised through anonymous, autonom- ised mechanisms. The paradox is accordingly included in the process as a permanent risk.
Luhmann now argues that this has to be rendered invisible. Form analysis shows that this is very crucially produced by the fetish character of the social forms, that is, through their reification. Thus the legal form does not even appear as a social relation to begin with. This lends the foundational paradox its stability. However, as the debate about the paradoxology of systems theory has also shown, subversion lurks in the very heart of the legal form itself: that some day the connection of legal operations will fail. Precisely because the legal form is politically overdetermined and does not constitute a reversible principle, and because it has to be developed in their daily practices by actors who cannot initially understand it as their own practice - precisely for this reason its validity is deeply uncertain. 
The Institutionalisation of the Legal Form
In order to take the previous chapter’s insight that form analysis must not remain at this abstract level into account, it is firstly necessary to look into the historical possibility of change through practice using a dual structural theory. For this allows us to arrive at a lower level of abstraction that makes it methodologically possible to research concrete social formations or institutional ensembles.
Social forms constitute a central structuring principle of capitalist socialisation. Structuring principles are the structural elements which have the greatest time-space extensions.  They are the organising principles that specify types of societies by constituting an institutional fabric. Those interwoven institutional ensembles are in turn, following Anthony Giddens, the most fundamental identifying characteristics of a society.  Christoph Görg has pointed out that in this concept Giddens omits precisely social forms as a specific type of the autonomisation of structural moments.  Indeed, Giddens specifies that the distinguishing structural principle of class societies under modern capitalism is the disembedding of state and economic institutions, although they remain interconnected.  On closer inspection, he thus begins one abstraction level too low, namely already on that of institutions. Analytically it would rather be appropriate to speak of political, economic and legal forms. Following this shift, it is now possible to examine the relationship of structural principles and institutions. 
institutions is always concerned with stability’.5 Joachim Hirsch therefore says that social forms materialise in institutions.6 One could also phrase this following Maihofer as the former gaining a ‘material existence’ in the latter: The form requires an institutional base for its realisation, although it can oppose this base through the antagonisms embedded within it.7 For the procedures of the legal form this means that they materialise in various different temporal and spatial ways in the institutions of the specific social formations: in the German Federal Constitutional Court unlike the European Court of Justice or the US Supreme Court. In addition, the various sub-disciplines of private and public law are historical expressions of the general legal form. Social forms thus depend on the concrete manner of their institutionalisation and only exist in the institutional apparatuses, form-analytically speaking as ‘form II’.”翻译为中文

2.44.3 霸权去悖论化

在这一点上，我们在法律形式内部遇到了它的悖论，它处理的矛盾：司法论证是一种去悖论化的调节。法律形式的自主特性建立在不稳定的基础上。法律与非法的界定不是由法律形式本身预先决定的，而是霸权斗争的结果，这些斗争通过教条学的形式强制而被规范化。霸权领导权被写入了这种论证形式。特别是当我们询问为什么特定论点在法学中占据主导地位而不是另一个论点时，例如如何为“企业权利”进行辩护，而不是“工作权利”，法律形式的政治特性就显露出来。基于权力的决策最终通过论证形式得到保障，这些决策绝非任意。它们一方面遵循法律形式的教条要求，另一方面遵循可以被普遍化的霸权项目。因此，它们绝不是国家个体行为者的任意决定。

从形式上来看，霸权在法律中的铭刻表现在，教条论证的一致性总是只在法律形式的关系性自主中相对存在。因为论点本质上是基于它们对先前论点的重复——然而，完全相同的重复是不可能的。因此，在此处就已经可能发生霸权转移。卢曼设想的那种彻底停止质疑的方式是不可能的，他停留在“沟通理论的字面意义”上，并将解释作为以旧欧洲风格正确解读文本。教条学论证反而诞生了文本，而文本增加了说“不”的机会。霸权决策只是暂时解决了悖论。在对抗性斗争中，社会行为者网络设法以共识的方式将特定的世界观普遍化，这可以铭记在法律形式中。然而，由于决策的对抗性结构，逆霸权项目总是已经在计划之中：“每一个霸权秩序都可能受到逆霸权实践的挑战，即试图解构现有秩序以安装另一种形式的霸权”。n2
因此，霸权项目可以在法律形式上发挥作用，因为在这里，特别是法律知识工作者开发了特定的法律论证项目。因此，法律允许多元项目的统一。它几乎提供了一个理想类型的形式凝聚框架，因为它在自身结构中、其抽象性和对形式化论证的依赖上设计为普遍性。

2.44.4 自主化的物质支持

根据卢曼的观点，强制性决策和法律效力——在法院中的制度化——合理地支持了法律的自主化。法院最终必须做出决策，并至少暂时实际上“解放”这些决策免于持续质疑，这是因为只有在个别案件中成功时法律形式才能成立。因为只有那时，法律操作的递归网络才能达到闭环。一个未被应用的法律会在某个时刻离开循环。因此，司法权在法律系统内并非仅与卢曼有关，而是对汉斯·凯尔森而言已获得特殊地位。他正确强调，“不应忽视一个重要事实，即法律最终不是立法者或多或少清晰地规定的，也不是习惯法或多或少含糊地暗示的。法律是法院最终做出的决定”。114 实际上，许多（如果不是大多数）法律沟通发生在司法机构之外；然而，这只发生在确保它们始终也可以接受司法程序的物质背景下。法律约束性决策在“最后实例”结束 - 一个可能无限回归的过程，这将破坏法律话语之间的联系，从而使自主化变得不可能。

显然，这些决策又是新的文本，其含义无法一劳永逸地固定，而是开放于霸权实践。然而，“最后实例”的决策至少在历史时刻固定了特定含义，使其能够生效。它为特定时刻“冻结”了社会关系，从而允许随之发展的决策。最初意义上的转变被暂时推迟。伴随法律效力的拒绝司法拒绝权真正是法律形式的支柱。因此，作为其制度化的法院对法律关系性自主尤为重要。没有强大的司法制度化（包括广泛的起诉权和管辖权），这种自主性无法发展。强大的行为者可以改变“合法”含义并引发一种法宝[Legendenrecht]。

2.44.5 物化与悖论
管理社会形式的悖论具有处理社会矛盾的效果。矛盾（基础悖论）并没有被解决，而是被维持。没有总体规划或控制主体，“无窗单体”通过匿名、自主化的机制进行去中心化的连接。相应地，悖论被包含在过程中作为一个永久风险。

卢曼现在认为，这必须被隐去。形式分析表明，这在很大程度上是由社会形式的物化特性产生的，即通过它们的物化。因此，法律形式甚至最初不会出现为一种社会关系。这赋予了基础悖论以稳定性。然而，正如系统理论悖论学的辩论也表明的那样，法律形式本身的核心潜藏着颠覆性：总有一天，法律操作的连接会失败。正因为法律形式在政治上过度决定，不构成一个可逆转的原则，而且因为它必须由最初无法将其视为他们自己实践的行为者在日常实践中发展——正因为这个原因，它的有效性极为不确定。

法律形式的制度化
为了考虑到前一章的洞见，即形式分析不得停留在这一抽象层面上，首先需要使用双重结构理论来探究通过实践带来变革的历史可能性。因为这使我们能够到达更低的抽象层面，从而在方法上有可能研究具体的社会构成或制度集合。

社会形式构成资本主义社会化的一个中心结构原则。结构原则是具有最大时空扩展的结构元素。它们是指定社会类型的组织原则，构成了制度网络。安东尼·吉登斯认为，这些交织的制度集合反过来是社会最根本的鉴别特征。克里斯托夫·格尔指出，在这一概念中，吉登斯正好遗漏了社会形式作为特定类型的结构时刻自主化。确实，吉登斯指出，现代资本主义下阶级社会的区别结构原则是国家和经济机构的脱嵌，尽管它们仍然相互联系。仔细检查，他因此开始于过低的抽象层面，即已是制度层面。分析上更恰当的是谈论政治、经济和法律形式。遵循这一转变，现在可以考察结构原则和制度的关系。

制度总是与稳定性有关。5 因此，约阿希姆·希尔什说社会形式在制度中具体化。6 也可以遵循迈霍弗的说法，将前者在后者中获得“物质存在”：形式需要制度基础来实现，尽管它可以通过其中嵌入的对立来反对这一基础。7 对于法律形式的程序而言，这意味着它们在特定社会构成的制度中以各种不同的时间和空间方式具体化：在德国联邦宪法法院与欧洲法院或美国最高法院不同。此外，私法和公法的各个子学科是一般法律形式的历史表达。社会形式因此取决于它们制度化的具体方式，只存在于制度装置中，从形式分析上讲，作为“形式 II”。

请将“1.1	The Intersection of Different Structural Principles
Social forms are structural principles specific to capitalism. They in no way explain the entire institutional system of those societies. If one wants to do justice to the diversity of modern technologies of domination, one has to acknowledge that modern societies ‘were not constructed on a political tabula rasa but on the historically variable foundations of past social forms and discourses’.8 Thus, for example, ‘an inherited patriarchal context’9 exists which is reconfigured within capitalism. In the institutions the legal form links up with other structural principles, that is, with other social forms, power technologies and ways of subjectivation, which also inscribe themselves in the apparatuses. Therefore it is no accident that Pashukanis addresses the close connection of law and commodity production. Civil law contracts are always a combination of the legal and the commodity form. At the same time the legal form is institutionally entangled with the political form in a particular manner. But also the gender binary and heteronormativity articulate themselves through legal practice. It is only in bourgeois society that nature and society are to such an extent - and with such subtlety - subjected to a binary gen-dering and at the same time naturalised; and as such it is only here that the traces of social construction are entirely eliminated.10 The hierarchical gender binary is thus the way in which patriarchal and heterosexist relations of dom- 
ination are inscribed in modern institutions and systematically interlink in them with the legal form. This connection could mainly emerge in a process of searching, because the legal form operates simultaneously in an individualising and a totalising manner. On the one hand it sanctions existing social ways of subjectivation, on the other it brings them together into a hegemonic whole through the very abstraction it performs, and thus also processes its antagonistic position. Broadening the perspective to not only examine the materialisation of the legal form in the institutions, but also in the totality of socially dispersed legal practices, one can speak of legal dispositifs. Those encompass more than the condensation of the legal form in the institutions alone; they also articulate discourses about law that are not yet institutionalised, attempts to make a certain discourse hegemonic, and everyday practices.
The totality of social dispositifs forms a historical block, that is, a coherent ensemble of techniques of coercive force and consensus that reproduces itself over a longer period of time. The perspective of analysis depends on which phenomena one seeks to investigate. All that matters is to reflect carefully on which level of abstraction to argue at; on that of the legal form, the historical block, the dispositif or the institution.
1.2	Institutions and Everyday Behaviour
The further structural principles can extend across institutions in space and time, the more resistant they can become to attempts to change them.11 For institutions gradually develop a ‘longue durée’, which both pre-exists the lives of individuals born into a particular society and also outlasts them?2 We are, as John Holloway put it, born into a world that is always already pre-shaped ‘before we begin to reflect critically’?3 As they are institutionalised, structural principles are therefore immanent to everyday actions in a way that marks its ‘immediate perceptions and behavioural orientation’ (Hirsch). Angelika Wet- terer accordingly also speaks in the context of the construction of gender of a ‘relationship of concealment44 whereby the construction remains systematically hidden from those who develop it. For in routine everyday behaviour, relevant knowledge for activities, practical consciousness, is immune to the institutionalising structural principles that are already given from the outset?5 Due to the longue durée those become plausible in the world of everyday life. Here the connection of the overall ‘spectrality’ of social conditions and their continual recreation in everyday behaviour - until they are ‘unburdened’ through their sedimentation in institutions - becomes clear.
1.3	Apparatuses
In addition to social forms and other technologies of power, there still remains to be examined materiality. The concept of the ‘apparatus’ allows both Poulantzas and Foucault, following Althusser, to describe the materiality and the external unity of certain practices. It appears therefore appropriate to follow Foucault and separate institutions analytically into apparatuses on the one hand and power technologies on the other. Here power technologies thus describe the respective effects of power (subjectivation, discipline, social cohesion, etc.), while the apparatuses represent the mechanisms of the internal momentum required. Ultimately institutions are also nothing other than social practices, but they temporarily fix those practices. For example, as the processes of the legal form reproduce and thus institutionalise themselves permanently through routinised practices, as they develop a differentiated court system, specialised jurists, a plurality of codified legal norms, a specific practice of legal argumentation and of filtering non-juridical ‘reality’, norm projections, legality and illegality etc., a legal world of its own emerges and gives rise to an interest in its own perpetuation, which is then articulated in the social practices of the legal actors, and most notably in bureaucracy.
That historically and spatially diverse legal apparatuses can come into conflict with each other and are marked by social contradictions, is plausibly described by Poulantzas. An interconnected ensemble of diverse apparatuses can only be created through a hegemonic project; it then shapes a specific social formation. It is, however, always possible to act in opposition to institutions, because their materiality is neither eternal nor insurmountable. There always remains ‘scope for actions to overflow or circumvent structural con- straints’.16 Their success is nonetheless less likely and significantly harder, in particular for those who are disadvantaged by strategic selectivity. Moreover strategic and even routinised action can fail: ‘[b]ecause subjects are never unitary, never fully aware of the conditions of strategic action, never fully equipped to realise their preferred strategies, and always face possible opposition from actors pursuing other strategies or tactics’?7
On the Relationship of Law and State
The legal form is not only relationally autonomous with regard to the economic, but also to the political form. As can be seen especially in Blanke et. al., law’s own density is recognised in the first, but not in the second case. It is therefore nothing but the medium through which the state can act upon society. Poulantzas’ theory is similarly characterised by something of a ‘statesurplus’. The reason is easy to see: modern, rational law and the modern nation state are deeply interwoven. Thus all legal institutions are organised by the state and the necessary enforceability of the law, the repressive side of legal technology, is tied to an extra-economic coercive force. At the same time the legal form limits the immediate coercive force of the state.18 Luhmann holds that it is the obvious mutual dependence of the two systems, politics and law, that makes it difficult to discover their functional differentiation?9 It is this institutional symbiosis that also leads to a legal-theoretical dilemma for materialist theory, namely to the inadequate analysis of the legal form. The impression is created that law is always implicitly included in the context of state-theoretical reflections. However, this actually negates its own materiality, which cannot be reduced to that of the state.
In order to make this ‘differentiation’ visible, I propose to distinguish between the necessary articulation of political form and legal form on the most abstract analytical level, that of structural principles, on the one hand, and the historically and spatially specific institutional form of law and nation state on the other. Those levels of analysis must not be mixed with each other, because then historical forms would be taken for structural principles.
Already on the level of the structural principles of capitalist societies it must be noted that both forms function independently, but at the same time refer to each other. The legal form is always also a process of reflection, in which every practice develops a relationship to itself. In specialist legal literature and comments or in court decisions, for example, questions of the attribution of actions to subject artefacts or collisions of social norms are discussed. Law is here a specific technology for reflection, which captures and legally encloses concrete social practices in the autonomous procedures of legal arguments. Law thus brings those practices together in a hegemonic order, and formalises them. It thus allows one to define a position that can then be again politically contested.20 In terms of its content, the political form processes the art of behaviour management and of governing the population. As Pashukanis shows, its formal democratic procedures reflect legal norms publicly. While the legal form thus codifies social practices and the norms arising from them juridically and brings them into a certain operationalisable form, the political form confronts in its processes the norms thus developed, with hegemonic struggles taking place in relation to the modalities of governance. Hegemony is inherent in different ways in both of the two forms.”翻译为中文

2.44.3 霸权去悖论化

在这一点上，我们在法律形式内遇到其悖论，即它处理的矛盾：司法论证是一种去悖论化的调节。法律形式的自主特征建立在不稳固的基础上。什么是合法的，进而什么是非法的，并不是由法律形式本身预设的，而是霸权斗争的结果，这些斗争通过教条学的形式强制正常化。霸权领导权被写入了这种论证方式。特别是当我们询问为何正是这个特定论点在法理上占据优势而不是其他论点时，例如怎样为“企业权”辩护而不是“工作权”，法律形式的政治性就揭示出来。基于权力的决策，最终通过论证形式获得保障，这些决策绝非任意。它们一方面遵循法律形式的教条要求，另一方面遵循可以普遍化的霸权项目。因此，它们绝不是国家个体行为者的任意决策。

从形式上看，霸权在法律中的铭记体现在，教条论证的一致性始终只是相对的，取决于法律形式的关系性自主。因为论点本质上基于对先前论点的重复——然而，完全相同的重复是不可能的。因此，霸权转移就可以在这个阶段发生。质疑无法以卢曼设想的那种彻底方式停止，他止步于“沟通理论的字面意义”，并将解释设定为以旧欧洲风格解读文本的正确注释。相反，教条学论证倾向于产生文本，而文本增加了说“不”的机会。霸权决策只是暂时解决了悖论。在对抗性斗争中，社会行为者网络设法以共识方式普遍化特定的世界观，从而能够铭记于法律形式中。然而，由于决策的对抗性结构，逆霸权项目总是在酝酿之中：“每个霸权秩序都可能被逆霸权实践——即旨在解构现有秩序以建立另一种霸权形式的实践——所挑战”。

霸权项目因此可以利用法律形式，因为在这里，特别是法律知识工作者以特定的法律论证方式发展项目。因此，法律允许分歧项目的统一。它几乎提供了理想类型的形式凝聚框架，因为它在自身结构、抽象性和对形式化论证的依赖上设计为通用性。

2.44.4 自主化的物质支持

根据卢曼的见解，强制性决策和法律效力——在法院中的制度化——正当地支持了法律的自主化。法院最终必须做出决策，并至少暂时实际“解放”这些决策免于持续质疑，这是因为只有在个别案件中成功时法律形式才能成立。因为只有那时，法律操作的递归网络才能完成闭环。未被应用的法律将在某个点上离开循环。因此，司法权并非仅在卢曼那里在法律系统内获得特殊地位，而是对汉斯·凯尔森而言也如此。他强调，“不应忽视一个重要事实，即从最终分析来看，法律不是立法者或多或少清晰地制定的，也不是习惯规则或多或少含糊地暗示的。法律是法院最终做出的决定”。114实际上，很多（如果不是大多数）法律沟通发生在司法机构之外；然而，这只发生在确保它们始终也可能受到司法程序处理的物质背景下。法律约束性决策在“最后实例”结束——一个可能无限回归的过程，这将破坏法律话语之间的联系，从而使自主化不可能。

显然，这些决策再次成为新的文本，其含义无法一劳永逸地固定，而是开发于霸权实践。然而，“最后实例”的决策至少在历史时刻固定了特定含义，从而使其能够生效。它为特定时刻“冻结”了社会关系，从而允许从中发展的决策。最初的意义转变被暂时推迟。伴随法律效力的司法拒绝禁令真正是法律形式的支柱。因此，作为其制度化的法院因此对法律的关系性自主特别重要。没有强大的司法制度化（包括广泛的诉讼权和管辖权），这种自主性无法发展。强大的行为者可以改变“合法”的含义，并引发某种法律传说[Lagendenrecht]。

2.44.5 物化与悖论
处理社会形式悖论的效果是处理社会矛盾。矛盾（基础悖论）正是没有被解决，而是被维持。通过匿名、自主化的机制去中心化地连接“无窗的单体”，没有总体规划或控制主体。因此，悖论作为一个永久性风险被纳入过程。

卢曼现在认为，这必须被隐去。形式分析表明，这在很大程度上是由社会形式的物化特性产生的，即通过它们的物化。因此，法律形式甚至最初看来都不是一种社会关系。这给了基础悖论以稳定性。然而，正如系统理论悖论学的辩论也表明的，法律形式本身的核心潜伏着颠覆性：某一天法律操作的连接将失败。正因为法律形式在政治上过度决定，不构成一个可逆原则，并且因为它必须由最初无法将其视为自己实践的行为者在日常实践中发展——正因为这个原因，其有效性深受质疑。

法律形式的制度化
为了考虑到前一章的洞见，即形式分析不应停留在这一抽象层面，因此首先需要通过使用双重结构理论探讨实践带来变革的历史可能性。这使我们能够达到更低的抽象层面，从而在方法上有能力研究具体的社会形态或制度集合。

社会形式是资本主义特有的结构原则。它们无法解释这些社会的整个制度体系。如果想要做到对现代统治技术的多样性公正，那么必须承认，现代社会“并非建立在政治白板上，而是建立在过去社会形式和话语的历史变化基础上”。因此，例如，“继承的父权背景”存在，并在资本主义内部被重新配置。在制度中，法律形式与其他结构原则相连接，即与其他社会形式、权力技术和主体化方式相联系，这些也在装置中体现自身。因此，帕舒卡尼斯强调法律与商品生产的密切联系并非偶然。民法合同始终是法律与商品形式的结合。同时，法律形式以特定方式与政治形式机构性地纠缠在一起。但是，性别二元性和异性恋规范性也通过法律实践表达自身。只有在资产阶级社会，自然与社会在如此程度上——并且如此微妙地——被二元性别化并同时自然化；因此，社会构建的痕迹被完全消除。层级性的性别二元性因此是父权和异性恋主义统治关系如何被纳入现代制度并系统地与法律形式相互联系的方式。这种连接主要是在探索过程中形成的，因为法律形式同时以个体化和全面化的方式运作。一方面，它认可现有的社会主体化方式，另一方面，通过它执行的抽象，把它们整合成霸权整体，并因此处理其对立性。扩大视角，不仅考察法律形式在制度中的具体化，而且考察在社会分散的法律实践中的总体，可以谈论法律装置。这些装置不仅仅包含法律形式在制度中的凝聚；它们还表达了尚未制度化的关于法律的话语，试图使某种话语霸权化，以及日常实践。

社会装置的总体形成了一个历史块，即一个一致的强制力量和共识技术集合，它在较长时间内自我复制。分析视角取决于所寻求研究的现象。重要的是要仔细思考在哪个抽象层面上进行论证；在法律形式、历史块、装置或制度的层面上。

制度与日常行为
结构原则能够在空间和时间上跨越制度的范围越广，它们就能变得对变革尝试的抵抗力越强。因为制度逐渐发展出一种“长期持续性”，既在特定社会中出生的个体生命之前存在，也超越他们的生命。正如约翰·霍洛威所说，我们出生在一个“在我们开始批判性反思之前”已经被预设形状的世界中。由于它们的制度化，结构原则因此以一种标记其“直接感知和行为取向”的方式内在于日常行动中。安吉丽卡·韦特雷尔在讨论性别构建的背景下相应地谈到了一种“隐藏关系”，其中构建对那些开发它的人系统性地保持隐蔽。因为在日常行为中，活动所需的相关知识、实践意识，免疫于从一开始就给定的制度化结构原则。由于长期持续性，这些在日常生活世界中变得合理化。在这里，社会条件整体“幽灵性”及其在日常行为中的持续再创造——直到它们通过在制度中的沉淀而“卸载”——的联系变得清晰。

装置
除了社会形式和其他权力技术之外，物质性仍需被考察。装置的概念使得帕鲁萨和福柯能够紧跟阿尔都塞描述某些实践的物质性和外部统一性。因此，遵循福柯的方式，在分析上将制度分为装置一方面和权力技术另一方面似乎是适当的。这里权力技术因此描述了各自的权力效应（主体化、纪律、社会凝聚等），而装置代表了所需的内部动力机制。归根结底，制度也不过是社会实践的集合，但它们暂时固定了这些实践。例如，随着法律形式通过例行实践的再生产和制度化，发展出一个分化的法院系统、专业法律人士、多样化的法典化法律规范、特定的法律论证和筛选非法律“现实”的实践、规范投射、合法性和非法性等，法律的自己的世界出现了，并产生了自我维持的兴趣，然后在法律行为者的社会实践中，尤其是在官僚机构中表达出来。

不同的法律装置在历史上和空间上可能相互冲突，并受社会矛盾的标记，这一点帕鲁萨的描述是合理的。一个多样化装置的相互联系的集合只能通过霸权项目来创建；然后它塑造了一个特定的社会形态。然而，由于它们的物质性既不是永恒的也不是不可克服的，总是有可能反对制度。总有“行动溢出或规避结构限制”的“空间”。16 它们的成功虽然不太可能且要困难得多，特别是对于那些受到战略选择性不利影响的人。此外，战略甚至例行行动也可能失败：“因为主体从不是统一的，对战略行动的条件从不完全了解，从不完全装备实现他们偏好的策略，并且总是面对其他策略或战术的行动者的可能反对。”17

关于法律与国家的关系
法律形式不仅相对于经济形式具有关系性自主，而且相对于政治形式也是如此。如布兰克等人特别指出的，法律自身的密度在第一种情况下被认可，但在第二种情况下没有。因此，它不过是国家对社会行使作用的媒介。帕鲁萨的理论同样被某种“国家过剩”所特征化。原因很容易看出：现代、理性的法
